channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Bharat Forge Builds ₹1,500 Cr Defence Hub to Boost Production
item.description: Bharat Forge's subsidiary, Agneyastra Energetics, has begun constructing a vast ₹1,500 crore defense manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh. The move aims to strengthen India's domestic capabilities in advanced energetics and defense systems. The company's order book is nearing ₹11,000 crore, reflecting strong growth in India's defense sector and the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.

item.title: Investor Yield Demands Force NABARD to Withdraw ₹7,000 Cr Bond
item.description: State-owned NABARD withdrew its ₹7,000-crore bond issuance after receiving bids for only ₹3,030 crore, as investors demanded higher yields. This move by a AAA-rated issuer highlights growing investor caution, market volatility, and expectations of tighter funding conditions in India's debt market, forcing even strong entities to reassess yield demands.

item.title: Berger Paints Stock Jumps on Q4 Beat, Margins Face Pressure
item.description: Berger Paints India's stock surged over 11% this week after strong March quarter earnings, boosted by 11.8% volume growth and a clearer competitive outlook. Analysts are positive about FY27, forecasting double-digit revenue growth. However, volatile raw material costs and the aggressive entry of players like Birla Opus present key concerns for profit margins.

item.title: SIA Profit Plunges 57% on Air India Losses, But India Growth Strategy Remains
item.description: Singapore Airlines (SIA) reported a 57.4% profit decrease for the fiscal year ending March 2026, heavily impacted by Air India's loss of over S$3.56 billion. Global issues like geopolitical instability, Pakistan's airspace closure, and rising fuel costs worsened Air India's performance. Despite these challenges, SIA sees its stake in Air India as key to tapping into India's rapidly growing aviation market, calling it a long-term strategic play.

item.title: Raghu Vamsi Plans ₹500 Cr Aerospace Plant in AP, Creating 3,000 Jobs
item.description: Raghu Vamsi Machine Tools is investing ₹500 crore to build a 100-acre aerospace and defense manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh. The project aims to create 3,000 jobs, focusing on micro turbojet engines, UAVs, and precision engineering to support India's self-reliance goals. While the company has seen revenue growth, its move into advanced defense technology faces challenges against established players.

item.title: Tata Steel Q4: Profit Climbs 9% But Costs and Charges Hit Results
item.description: Tata Steel announced robust sequential growth for Q4 FY26, with net profit rising 9% to ₹2,926 crore and revenue up 11% to ₹63,270 crore, driven by improved demand and operational efficiencies. The company also declared a final dividend of ₹4 per share and plans to acquire a 23% stake in TM International Logistics Ltd for ₹335 crore. However, higher raw material costs and a one-time charge related to its Netherlands unit led to a profit miss against analyst expectations, signaling underlying cost pressures despite a strong top-line performance.

item.title: Godrej Industries Profit Soars 142%, Stock Dips on Valuation Worries
item.description: Godrej Industries reported a strong Q4 FY26, with net profit surging 142% to ₹444 crore on 33.1% revenue growth to ₹7,694 crore. Operating income nearly doubled, and EBITDA margins expanded to 15.2%. However, the company's stock dipped over 2% during trading. The board also approved significant investments and fundraising for future expansion.

item.title: NABARD Buys ₹350.72 Cr BKC Campus, Deepens Financial Hub Footprint
item.description: NABARD acquired leasehold rights to a ₹350.72 crore commercial campus in Mumbai's Bandra-Kurla Complex (BKC) from MTNL. The 43,287 sq ft property, with 52 years remaining on its lease, positions NABARD within India's premier financial district. This move reflects public sector entities monetizing non-core real estate to fund operations, capitalizing on BKC's scarce supply and sustained institutional demand.
